summary.brief: Rodriguez v. Surgical Assocs.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: On April 16, 2012, Rodriguez was referred to a hospital in Lincoln, Nebraska, due to stomach pains, fever, and nausea.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Upon examining Rodriguez, Fitzke determined that she needed an immediate cholecystectomy, a surgical procedure to remove her gallbladder.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: While her gallbladder was being removed, it ruptured and released stones and purulent material, or pus, into Rodriguez’ abdominal cavity—an unavoidable risk of the surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rodriguez began experiencing significant pain and her status changed from outpatient to inpatient.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The surgical expert testified that Fitzke breached the standard of care in three instances: (1) by failing to follow the three-step protocol for treating septic shock, (2) by failing to create and follow a reasonable surgical differential diagnosis, and (3) by canceling the CT scan that had been ordered for Rodriguez on April 18, 2012.
evidence_index.speaker: Rodriguez's surgical expert

evidence_index.text: The critical care physician also testified that Fitzke’s canceling the CT scan and failing to timely treat the source of Rodriguez’ infections were a breach of the standard of care.
evidence_index.speaker: Rodriguez's critical care physician

evidence_index.text: Ultimately, Rodriguez had eight additional operations during the subsequent 11⁄2 months and remained hospitalized until July, with numerous complications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Because we do not find merit in Rodriguez’ claims, we affirm.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: We presume that the jury found for appellees on all issues presented to it.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient referred to hospital due to stomach pains, fever, and nausea. Examination revealed need for immediate cholecystectomy.

focused_exam.abdomen: Soft, tender, and distended; no signs of peritonitis initially

critical_findings: Gangrenous gallbladder attached to other organs

requestables.name: CT scan of abdomen
requestables.description: CT scan with unspecified contrast to evaluate abdominal pain and hypotension

reveal.result_summary: CT scan was canceled; no results available

requestables.name: X-ray of abdomen
requestables.description: X-ray to check for free air and lung infiltrates

reveal.result_summary: Free air in abdomen and new developing lobe infiltrates in left lower lung
reveal.decision_impact: Contributed to decision against immediate surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with stomach pains, fever, and nausea

timeline_phases.name: Postoperative Management
timeline_phases.description: Patient experiences significant pain, status changes to inpatient

decision_points.prompt: Should the CT scan be performed to investigate the cause of the patient's symptoms?

options.description: Proceed with CT scan of abdomen
options.clinical_reasoning: CT scan could identify complications such as intestinal perforation

options.description: Cancel CT scan and continue with current treatment
options.clinical_reasoning: Concerns about kidney function and pneumonia; CT scan deemed low yield

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Risk of contrast due to kidney function and pneumonia; low yield expected

expected_action_court.description: CT scan should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated CT scan was necessary to identify complications

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform CT scan delayed diagnosis of intestinal perforation
explanation.what_should_have_happened: CT scan would have identified the perforation earlier
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient required eight additional operations and remained hospitalized for 1.5 months

key_findings: Court found no merit in Rodriguez’ claims
Jury found for appellees on all issues

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: CT could have identified complications earlier

factual_timeline.event: Patient referred to hospital with stomach pains, fever, and nausea
Patient referred to hospital due to stomach pains

tests_performed.result: Free air in abdomen, lung infiltrates

court_findings.finding: No breach of duty by defendant
court_findings.legal_significance: Jury found for appellees on all issues
